  "findings": [
    "Belt mistracking caused by off-center loading and incorrect idler skew during active conveyor operation",
    "Potential for severe belt damage, material spillage, and friction-induced fire hazards",
    "Increased risk of worker entanglement or crushing if manual adjustments are attempted while the system is running"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately shut down and lock out/tag out (LOTO) the conveyor system",
    "Re-align and adjust the conveyor idlers according to CEMA standards",
    "Correct the chute design or loading mechanism to ensure material is loaded centrally on the belt",
    "Verify all guarding is compliant with OSHA 1910.212 before restarting the system"
  ],
  "rationale": "Active belt mistracking due to off-center loading and improper idler skew poses immediate safety risks, including friction fire hazards, material spillage, and catastrophic belt failure. The system must be shut down immediately under LOTO to correct the tracking and loading issues safely.",
  "references": [
    "CEMA (Conveyor Equipment Manufacturers Association) Belt Conveyors for Bulk Materials",
    "OSHA 1910.212 (General requirements for all machines)"
  ]
}
